verses.kjv: And the famine was sore in the land.
verses.ct: The famine was severe throughout the land.
verses.web: The famine was severe in the land.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, when they had eaten up the corn which they had brought out of Egypt, their father said unto them, Go again, buy us a little food.
verses.ct: Once they had used up all the grain they brought from Egypt, their father told them, "Go back and purchase a little more food for us."
verses.web: When they had eaten up the grain which they had brought out of Egypt, their father said to them, “Go again, buy us a little more food.”

verses.kjv: And Judah spake unto him, saying, The man did solemnly protest unto us, saying, Ye shall not see my face, except your brother be with you.
verses.ct: Judah replied, "The man warned us sternly, saying, 'You will not be allowed into my presence unless your brother is with you.'"
verses.web: Judah spoke to him, saying, “The man solemnly warned us, saying, ‘You shall not see my face, unless your brother is with you.’

verses.kjv: If thou wilt send our brother with us, we will go down and buy thee food:
verses.ct: "If you are willing to send our brother along with us, we will go down and buy food for you."
verses.web: If you’ll send our brother with us, we’ll go down and buy you food;

verses.kjv: But if thou wilt not send him, we will not go down: for the man said unto us, Ye shall not see my face, except your brother be with you.
verses.ct: "But if you refuse to send him, we will not go, because the man told us, 'You will not be allowed into my presence unless your brother is with you.'"
verses.web: but if you don’t send him, we won’t go down, for the man said to us, ‘You shall not see my face, unless your brother is with you.’ ”

verses.kjv: And Israel said, Wherefore dealt ye so ill with me, as to tell the man whether ye had yet a brother?
verses.ct: Israel said, "Why did you treat me so badly by telling the man you had another brother?"
verses.web: Israel said, “Why did you treat me so badly, telling the man that you had another brother?”

verses.kjv: And they said, The man asked us straitly of our state, and of our kindred, saying, Is your father yet alive? have ye another brother? and we told him according to the tenor of these words: could we certainly know that he would say, Bring your brother down?
verses.ct: They answered, "The man questioned us thoroughly about ourselves and our family, asking, 'Is your father still living? Do you have another brother?' We simply answered his questions. How could we have possibly known he would demand, 'Bring your brother here'?"
verses.web: They said, “The man asked directly concerning ourselves, and concerning our relatives, saying, ‘Is your father still alive? Have you another brother?’ We just answered his questions. Is there any way we could know that he would say, ‘Bring your brother down?’ ”

verses.kjv: And Judah said unto Israel his father, Send the lad with me, and we will arise and go; that we may live, and not die, both we, and thou, and also our little ones.
verses.ct: Judah said to his father Israel, "Let the boy come with me, and we will get up and go, so that we may survive and not starve — all of us, including you and our children."
verses.web: Judah said to Israel, his father, “Send the boy with me, and we’ll get up and go, so that we may live, and not die, both we, and you, and also our little ones.

verses.kjv: I will be surety for him; of my hand shalt thou require him: if I bring him not unto thee, and set him before thee, then let me bear the blame for ever:
verses.ct: "I personally guarantee his safety. You can hold me responsible. If I do not return him to you and place him right in front of you, I will carry the guilt for the rest of my life."
verses.web: I’ll be collateral for him. From my hand will you require him. If I don’t bring him to you, and set him before you, then let me bear the blame forever;

verses.kjv: For except we had lingered, surely now we had returned this second time.
verses.ct: "If we had not delayed so long, we could have already made the trip and come back a second time by now."
verses.web: for if we hadn’t delayed, surely we would have returned a second time by now.”

verses.kjv: And their father Israel said unto them, If it must be so now, do this; take of the best fruits in the land in your vessels, and carry down the man a present, a little balm, and a little honey, spices, and myrrh, nuts, and almonds:
verses.ct: Their father Israel told them, "If that is how it must be, then do this: pack some of the finest products of the land in your bags and bring them as a gift to the man — a little balm, a little honey, spices, myrrh, nuts, and almonds."
verses.web: Their father, Israel, said to them, “If it must be so, then do this: Take from the choice fruits of the land in your bags, and carry down a present for the man, a little balm, a little honey, spices and myrrh, nuts, and almonds;

verses.kjv: And take double money in your hand; and the money that was brought again in the mouth of your sacks, carry it again in your hand; peradventure it was an oversight:
verses.ct: "Also take twice as much silver with you, and return the silver that was placed back in the tops of your sacks. Perhaps it was a mistake."
verses.web: and take double money in your hand, and take back the money that was returned in the mouth of your sacks. Perhaps it was an oversight.

verses.kjv: Take also your brother, and arise, go again unto the man:
verses.ct: "Take your brother as well, and go back to the man."
verses.web: Take your brother also, get up, and return to the man.

verses.kjv: And God Almighty give you mercy before the man, that he may send away your other brother, and Benjamin. If I be bereaved of my children, I am bereaved.
verses.ct: "May God Almighty grant you compassion in the man's presence, so that he releases your other brother along with Benjamin. As for me, if I must lose my children, then I must lose them."
verses.web: May God Almighty give you mercy before the man, that he may release to you your other brother and Benjamin. If I am bereaved of my children, I am bereaved.”

verses.kjv: And the men took that present, and they took double money in their hand, and Benjamin; and rose up, and went down to Egypt, and stood before Joseph.
verses.ct: So the men gathered the gifts, took twice the amount of silver, and brought Benjamin with them. They traveled down to Egypt and presented themselves before Joseph.
verses.web: The men took that present, and they took double money in their hand, and Benjamin; and got up, went down to Egypt, and stood before Joseph.

verses.kjv: And when Joseph saw Benjamin with them, he said to the ruler of his house, Bring these men home, and slay, and make ready; for these men shall dine with me at noon.
verses.ct: When Joseph noticed Benjamin among them, he instructed the manager of his household, "Take these men to my home, slaughter an animal, and prepare a meal, because these men will eat with me at midday."
verses.web: When Joseph saw Benjamin with them, he said to the steward of his house, “Bring the men into the house, and butcher an animal, and prepare; for the men will dine with me at noon.”

verses.kjv: And the man did as Joseph bade; and the man brought the men into Joseph’s house.
verses.ct: The manager carried out Joseph's orders and brought the men to Joseph's house.
verses.web: The man did as Joseph commanded, and the man brought the men to Joseph’s house.

verses.kjv: And the men were afraid, because they were brought into Joseph’s house; and they said, Because of the money that was returned in our sacks at the first time are we brought in; that he may seek occasion against us, and fall upon us, and take us for bondmen, and our asses.
verses.ct: The men became frightened when they were led into Joseph's house. They said to each other, "It is because of the silver that was put back in our sacks the first time. He is looking for an excuse to attack us, seize us as slaves, and take our donkeys."
verses.web: The men were afraid, because they were brought to Joseph’s house; and they said, “Because of the money that was returned in our sacks the first time, we’re brought in; that he may seek occasion against us, attack us, and seize us as slaves, along with our donkeys.”

verses.kjv: And they came near to the steward of Joseph’s house, and they communed with him at the door of the house,
verses.ct: They approached the steward of Joseph's household and spoke with him at the entrance of the house.
verses.web: They came near to the steward of Joseph’s house, and they spoke to him at the door of the house,

verses.kjv: And said, O sir, we came indeed down at the first time to buy food:
verses.ct: They said, "Please, sir, we came down the first time simply to purchase food."
verses.web: and said, “Oh, my lord, we indeed came down the first time to buy food.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, when we came to the inn, that we opened our sacks, and, behold, every man’s money was in the mouth of his sack, our money in full weight: and we have brought it again in our hand.
verses.ct: "When we stopped for the night and opened our sacks, each of us found his full amount of silver right at the top of his sack. We have brought it all back with us."
verses.web: When we came to the lodging place, we opened our sacks, and behold, each man’s money was in the mouth of his sack, our money in full weight. We have brought it back in our hand.

verses.kjv: And other money have we brought down in our hands to buy food: we cannot tell who put our money in our sacks.
verses.ct: "We have also brought additional silver to buy food. We have no idea who placed our silver in our sacks."
verses.web: We have brought down other money in our hand to buy food. We don’t know who put our money in our sacks.”

verses.kjv: And he said, Peace be to you, fear not: your God, and the God of your father, hath given you treasure in your sacks: I had your money. And he brought Simeon out unto them.
verses.ct: The steward said, "Everything is fine. Do not be afraid. Your God, the God of your father, must have placed treasure in your sacks. I received your payment." Then he brought Simeon out to them.
verses.web: He said, “Peace be to you. Don’t be afraid. Your God, and the God of your father, has given you treasure in your sacks. I received your money.” He brought Simeon out to them.

verses.kjv: And the man brought the men into Joseph’s house, and gave them water, and they washed their feet; and he gave their asses provender.
verses.ct: The steward led the men into Joseph's house, provided them with water so they could wash their feet, and gave feed to their donkeys.
verses.web: The man brought the men into Joseph’s house, and gave them water, and they washed their feet. He gave their donkeys fodder.

verses.kjv: And they made ready the present against Joseph came at noon: for they heard that they should eat bread there.
verses.ct: They prepared their gifts for Joseph's arrival at noon, since they had been told they would be eating a meal there.
verses.web: They prepared the present for Joseph’s coming at noon, for they heard that they should eat bread there.

verses.kjv: And when Joseph came home, they brought him the present which was in their hand into the house, and bowed themselves to him to the earth.
verses.ct: When Joseph arrived home, they presented the gifts they had brought into the house and bowed down before him with their faces to the ground.
verses.web: When Joseph came home, they brought him the present which was in their hand into the house, and bowed themselves down to the earth before him.

verses.kjv: And he asked them of their welfare, and said, Is your father well, the old man of whom ye spake? Is he yet alive?
verses.ct: He asked how they were doing and said, "Is your elderly father well, the one you told me about? Is he still alive?"
verses.web: He asked them of their welfare, and said, “Is your father well, the old man of whom you spoke? Is he yet alive?”

verses.kjv: And they answered, Thy servant our father is in good health, he is yet alive. And they bowed down their heads, and made obeisance.
verses.ct: They replied, "Your servant, our father, is in good health and still living." Then they knelt down and bowed low before him.
verses.web: They said, “Your servant, our father, is well. He is still alive.” They bowed down humbly.

verses.kjv: And he lifted up his eyes, and saw his brother Benjamin, his mother’s son, and said, Is this your younger brother, of whom ye spake unto me? And he said, God be gracious unto thee, my son.
verses.ct: Joseph looked up and saw his brother Benjamin, his own mother's son, and asked, "Is this your youngest brother, the one you mentioned to me?" Then he said, "May God be gracious to you, my son."
verses.web: He lifted up his eyes, and saw Benjamin, his brother, his mother’s son, and said, “Is this your youngest brother, of whom you spoke to me?” He said, “God be gracious to you, my son.”

verses.kjv: And Joseph made haste; for his bowels did yearn upon his brother: and he sought where to weep; and he entered into his chamber, and wept there.
verses.ct: Joseph hurried out because his emotions overwhelmed him at the sight of his brother. He found a private room and wept there.
verses.web: Joseph hurried, for his heart yearned over his brother; and he sought a place to weep. He entered into his room, and wept there.

verses.kjv: And he washed his face, and went out, and refrained himself, and said, Set on bread.
verses.ct: After washing his face, he came back out, composed himself, and said, "Serve the meal."
verses.web: He washed his face, and came out. He controlled himself, and said, “Serve the meal.”

verses.kjv: And they set on for him by himself, and for them by themselves, and for the Egyptians, which did eat with him, by themselves: because the Egyptians might not eat bread with the Hebrews; for that is an abomination unto the Egyptians.
verses.ct: Joseph was served separately, the brothers were served separately, and the Egyptians who ate with him were served separately, because Egyptians considered it detestable to share a meal with Hebrews.
verses.web: They served him by himself, and them by themselves, and the Egyptians who ate with him by themselves, because the Egyptians don’t eat with the Hebrews, for that is an abomination to the Egyptians.

verses.kjv: And they sat before him, the firstborn according to his birthright, and the youngest according to his youth: and the men marvelled one at another.
verses.ct: The brothers were seated facing him in order from the oldest to the youngest, and they looked at one another in astonishment.
verses.web: They sat before him, the firstborn according to his birthright, and the youngest according to his youth, and the men marveled with one another.

verses.kjv: And he took and sent messes unto them from before him: but Benjamin’s mess was five times so much as any of theirs. And they drank, and were merry with him.
verses.ct: Joseph had portions of food sent to them from his own table, but Benjamin's portion was five times larger than anyone else's. They drank freely and enjoyed themselves with him.
verses.web: He sent portions to them from before him, but Benjamin’s portion was five times as much as any of theirs. They drank, and were merry with him.
